source: .specs/agent-scenarios.md → Feature: Multiple agents in same workspace
notes: One opencode session and at least one different-adapter session driven concurrently in the SAME cwd by run-cell-multi.sh, observed by a single --record daemon. opencode lives in a ProcessOwnedStore (SQLite ~/.local/share/opencode/opencode.db) keyed by session.id; the partner adapters live in disjoint FilesUnderRoot/FilesUnderCWD transports, so the daemon labels each adapter independently and the workspace events.jsonl carries distinct sessions — one per adapter — each with its own working->ready arc and no cross-contamination. This opencode cell stores the opencode transcript for the replay golden and shares the workspace events.jsonl with the other cross_adapter cells. Every phase anchors on a WORKING transition, never a ready, because the process scanner emits a pre-session proc-<PID> row born in ready that never works — anchoring on working skips those placeholders, and new_session on agentB skips agentA's already-matched session. opencode's default model is a slow local LM Studio model (~30-45s/turn); it forces ready->working the instant its SQLite row appears, so opencode is the earliest working transition and binds agentA (its distinct, opencode-labeled working entry in the shared cwd is the observable). agentB is then the first DISTINCT new_session that enters working — a different-adapter partner — and agentB's full working->ready arc is the independent settle that proves the daemon never merges or flaps one adapter's row into the other's. The LOAD-BEARING assertion — two DISTINCT, independently-labeled per-adapter working arcs in one workspace plus an independent partner working->ready settle, with no merge/flap/cross-contamination — passes; daemon_capability=full (the prior `known_failing`/`bug` was a #476 defensive mislabel of opencode's generic headless fast-exit settle race, now reclassified as a caveat). CAVEAT: opencode's OWN working->ready settle truncates — single-shot `opencode run` exits ~140-190 ms after its final transcript_activity, before the ProcessOwnedStore poller reads the terminal step-finish=stop part (+several seconds), so no working->ready state_transition is emitted for the opencode row (basic-turn captures the same settle standalone when the poller tick wins the race). This is a settle-timing/driver property (cf. pi/turn-aborted-by-error headless fast-exit truncation), NOT a multi-adapter merge bug; the opencode row's trailing arc is therefore deliberately NOT asserted (agentA pins to opencode's working ENTRY only; the settle assertion lives on agentB, the partner). Asserts: two distinct sessions each entering working independently in one workspace, with a real partner working->ready arc.

text: First agent's turn drives ready -> working (binds agentA; anchoring on working skips every adapter's born-ready proc-<PID> placeholder). Wide window absorbs opencode's slow local model.

The OTHER agent — a DISTINCT session, different adapter, SAME cwd — drives ready -> working independently (new_session proves it is not agentA). Two agents, one workspace, each labeled with no cross-contamination.

agentB's turn completes working -> ready — an INDEPENDENT full arc on the second session, proving the daemon never merges or flaps one adapter's row into the other's. (opencode's OWN working->ready settle is the documented caveat: single-shot `opencode run` exits before the ProcessOwnedStore poller reads the terminal step-finish, so when opencode is the recorded session its trailing arc can truncate — see meta.notes. agentA's working entry already proves opencode appears as a distinct labeled session in the shared cwd.)
